query: Write a Python function that validates an email address. It should check for @ symbol, domain, and proper format. Return True if valid, False otherwise.
reference_answer: function with regex or string checks that validates email format

rubrics.name: correctness
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly validate standard email formats (user@domain.com) and reject invalid ones (missing @, no domain)

rubrics.name: edge_cases
rubrics.rubric: Must handle edge cases: subdomains (user@sub.domain.com), plus addressing (user+tag@domain.com), dots in local part

rubrics.name: code_quality
rubrics.rubric: Must be clean, readable Python with proper function signature and docstring

rubrics.name: efficiency
rubrics.rubric: Should not use excessive regex or unnecessary complexity

query: Write a Python function that takes a list of dictionaries and returns a new list sorted by a specified key. Handle missing keys gracefully by putting those items at the end.
reference_answer: function that sorts list of dicts by key with missing key handling

rubrics.name: correctness
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly sort dictionaries by the specified key

rubrics.name: missing_keys
rubrics.rubric: Must handle missing keys by placing those items at the end of the sorted list

rubrics.name: flexibility
rubrics.rubric: Should support both ascending and descending sort via parameter

rubrics.name: code_quality
rubrics.rubric: Must be clean Python with proper function signature

query: Write a Python class that implements a simple in-memory cache with TTL (time-to-live) support. Each entry should expire after the specified TTL seconds.
reference_answer: Cache class with get/set methods and TTL expiration

rubrics.name: correctness
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly store and retrieve values, and expire entries after TTL

rubrics.name: ttl_implementation
rubrics.rubric: Must check expiration on get() and clean up expired entries

rubrics.name: thread_safety
rubrics.rubric: Should handle concurrent access safely (threading.Lock or similar)

rubrics.name: api_design
rubrics.rubric: Must have clean API: get(key), set(key, value, ttl), delete(key), clear()

query: Write a Python function that parses a CSV string and returns a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents a row with column headers as keys.
reference_answer: function that parses CSV string to list of dicts

rubrics.name: correctness
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly parse CSV with headers and data rows

rubrics.name: edge_cases
rubrics.rubric: Must handle quoted fields with commas, empty fields, and different delimiters

rubrics.name: error_handling
rubrics.rubric: Must handle malformed CSV gracefully (missing columns, extra commas)

rubrics.name: code_quality
rubrics.rubric: Must be clean Python with proper function signature

query: Write a Python decorator that rate-limits function calls. It should allow N calls per time window and raise an exception if the limit is exceeded.
reference_answer: decorator with rate limiting using time tracking

rubrics.name: correctness
rubrics.rubric: Must correctly track calls and enforce the rate limit

rubrics.name: time_window
rubrics.rubric: Must use a sliding or fixed time window for rate limiting

rubrics.name: error_handling
rubrics.rubric: Must raise a clear exception when rate limit is exceeded

rubrics.name: flexibility
rubrics.rubric: Should work as a decorator with configurable parameters
